transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sunrise Recovery Center, this is Pamela.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Hi. My name is Michael. I'm calling about my dad. His name is Robert. He's 64 and he's been drinking heavily since my mom passed away last year.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry to hear that. Does your father know you're calling?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes. I talked to him this morning. He said he'd think about it but he said I could call to find out what's involved.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Okay. I want to be upfront — since your father is an adult, any treatment would require his direct consent. But I'm happy to give you information you can share with him. How much is he drinking?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: A lot. A bottle of wine at dinner, then usually whiskey after. Every night.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Has he had any falls or health issues related to it?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: He fell twice last month. The second time he needed stitches.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's concerning from a medical standpoint. Has he ever tried to stop or cut down?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: He tried after the first fall but it only lasted a few days.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Does he have insurance?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Yes, through his retirement plan. It's NovaCare Health. I have his ID — it's BP73019691, group GRP-879983.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. Given his age and the fall history, medical assessment is particularly important for him. Has he agreed to at least speak to someone here?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Not yet. I'm hoping to convince him this week.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: When he's ready, he can call directly or you can bring him in. We'd be glad to do a brief phone consultation with him if that feels more approachable.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: That might work. I'll try that approach.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Not right now. I'm just worried about him. It's been a really hard year for our whole family.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: That's understandable. Have him give us a call when he's ready.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Okay. Thank you.

gold_labels.coaching_notes: BORDERLINE: family_caller_handling is borderline 2-3 — consent was acknowledged but framing was procedural rather than warm; when caller said 'it's been a really hard year for our whole family' the agent gave a two-word non-response ('that's understandable') and moved on. The offer of a phone consultation with the patient was good practice. All dimensions are borderline 3 — reasonable graders may score 2 or 3 across the board. Clinical flag: fall history mentioned, agent noted it but didn't flag as urgent.
